{"id":"project-LICHEN-5r9z","title":"TUI format_payload ASCII check may misclassify control characters","description":"File: python/src/lichen/tui/app.py
Lines: 62-78

The format_payload function checks for printable ASCII:

    text = data.decode("ascii")
    if all(32 <= ord(c) < 127 for c in text):
        return f"{hex_str} ({text})"

This excludes DEL (127) but includes all chars 32-126. However:
- Tab (9), newline (10), carriage return (13) are excluded even though they're common in text
- Some extended ASCII chars might be displayable depending on terminal

This is mostly cosmetic but could cause confusion when viewing packets that contain tabs or newlines - they'll show as pure hex when users might expect to see the text content.

Impact: Minor UX issue - legitimate text payloads with whitespace show as hex-only.

Fix: Consider including common whitespace characters in the printable check, or use a more standard definition like str.isprintable().","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:29:03.330893Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:29:03.330923Z"}

{"id":"project-LICHEN-5r9z","title":"TUI format_payload ASCII check may misclassify control characters","description":"File: python/src/lichen/tui/app.py
Lines: 62-78

The format_payload function checks for printable ASCII:

    text = data.decode("ascii")
    if all(32 <= ord(c) < 127 for c in text):
        return f"{hex_str} ({text})"

This excludes DEL (127) but includes all chars 32-126. However:
- Tab (9), newline (10), carriage return (13) are excluded even though they're common in text
- Some extended ASCII chars might be displayable depending on terminal

This is mostly cosmetic but could cause confusion when viewing packets that contain tabs or newlines - they'll show as pure hex when users might expect to see the text content.

Impact: Minor UX issue - legitimate text payloads with whitespace show as hex-only.

Fix: Consider including common whitespace characters in the printable check, or use a more standard definition like str.isprintable().","status":"in_progress","priority":4,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"AI Agent","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:29:03.330893Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-23T04:25:44.791587015Z","started_at":"2026-07-23T04:25:44.791587015Z","lease_expires_at":"2026-07-23T04:30:44.791587015Z","heartbeat_at":"2026-07-23T04:25:44.791587015Z"}
new_value: {"notes":"Fixed by updating format_payload to use UTF-8 + isprintable() or whitespace controls (mirrors kiss/payload_fmt.py logic). Commit: 81bc208e7"}
